title: A Shell Hat and Jingle Bells
summary: When Juliesta the yellow-furred mouse is found crying in the local treehouse, her friends Mickey, Minnie, and Mole race to Hattie the mouse for assistance. They decide to craft a unique orange and pink cardboard hat, decorated with a shell and a yellow feather, to cheer her up. After a daring journey through the treetops to deliver the gift, they find Juliesta asleep. Upon waking her with a song, the group learns that her tears weren't for a lack of a hat, but due to a case of stage fright before a big show.

highlightQuote.text: Maybe Juliesta wants a hat. That would make her more happier.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want a story about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ah yes, a story about Hattie the mouse.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: In the garden with Mickey and Minnie and Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ah, Hattie and Mickey and Minnie and Mole, okay. Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse heard some commotion out in her front yard. Bang, bang, bang! Crash, crash, crash! And she thought, 'Oh my goodness, I'll just have one more bite of breakfast,'—nom, nom, nom—'and then go outside and see what it was.' And so she opened her door and peeked out, and she saw, running as fast as they could run, Mickey and Minnie and their friend Mole coming to see her. And so what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie said, 'Oh, good morning everybody. Um, what can I do for you?' And Mickey said, (heavy breathing). 'Oh,' said Hattie, 'You must have been running a long way. I hope you're okay. Would you like to have a seat?' (heavy breathing), said Mickey. 'Whew! We made it as fast as we could because (heavy breathing)...' And Hattie said, 'Minnie, what's Mickey talking about?' And Minnie said, (heavy breathing), 'We've been running because (heavy breathing)...' and Mole said, 'Oh my gosh.' And Hattie said, 'What's all this commotion?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (Giggles)

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mole said, 'Well, there is a little problem.' And Hattie said, 'Well what is it? Here, everyone sit on this lovely bench.' Because Hattie had a really nice bench in her garden in front of her house and they could all sit on it and have a cozy chat. And Minnie said, 'Let me tell you, we were just playing up in the treehouse and um, our friend...' what's their friend's name? It's Frazzle's cousin. What's Frazzle's cousin's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Juliesta.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Juliesta was crying! And she said, 'Wah, wah, wah!' And we tried to help her, you know, she was playing up in the treehouse with us, but then she got very upset and we said, 'Juliesta, what's the matter?' and she just said 'Wah!' So we didn't know what to do so we came right over to your house to ask for your help.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe Juliesta wants a hat. That would make her more happier.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Minnie said, 'Oh, that's an interesting idea. We could try that and see if that's what Juliesta needs. But what kind of a hat do you think she'd want, Mole?' And what did Mole say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Shell, cardboard, paint, feather.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a shell hat with cardboard and paint? Yeah, and a feather. And Hattie said, 'Wow, that sounds like a very nice hat idea. I'll get right to work on that. Why don't you come in and help me?' So everyone went into Hattie's workshop and they started to put together a beautiful hat for Juliesta. And after a few minutes, cutting and gluing and taping and painting, they had a cardboard hat with a shell and a feather. And what color was it painted?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Orange and pink.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and it was painted orange and pink. And they thought, 'Wow, Juliesta's really going to like this.' And Hattie said, 'Wait, what color is Juliesta's fur? I assume she has fur if she's related to Frazzle. What color's her fur?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yellow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's yellow? Oh, I see. Okay. Well, then I think this hat will go with her fur beautifully because it's orange and pink and shell and feather. It's a yellow feather too? So it'll be a perfect match. Well, let's bring it over. And so Hattie took the new hat they had made, and Hattie and Mickey and Minnie and Mole set out across the way to the little treehouse that was high up in a tree. And how did they get up to the treehouse? Did they climb a ladder or climb the tree next to the treehouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They climbed the tree next to the treehouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Right, because that's how you go up to this treehouse. You have to go up the tree next door and then go out across a branch and then swing down on top of the treehouse and then go through a little trapdoor in the roof. And Mole went first. He said, 'Okay, I got this guys.' And he scrambled across and dropped down onto the roof, and then he perked his head up and listened. What was he listening for?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He wasn't listening for anything, he was just putting the hat on Juliesta.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, but Juliesta was inside, so he poked his head inside the treehouse. He was listening to see if she was still crying, but he didn't hear anything. And so he poked his head down, and there he saw a little bundle of yellow fur curled up in the corner. And you know what it was doing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sleeping.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Sleeping. And Mole whispered, 'Okay, come on everybody, Juliesta's sleeping. We can come on in.' And so Mickey and Minnie and Hattie all climbed into the treehouse and they set down their new hat for Juliesta and they said, 'Okay, let's wake her up gently.' How did they wake her up?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Singing Jingle Bells. (Singing) Jingle bells, jingle bells, jingle all the way, oh what fun it is to ride in a one-horse open sleigh, hey! Jingle bells, jingle bells, jingle all the way...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And slowly, Juliesta started to wiggle her fur and shake, shake, shake just a tiny bit. And then her mouth opened—(yawn)—and then her eyes popped open and she looked around and said, 'Oh, who's that singing Jingle Bells? I almost forgot how sad I was because I love that song so much. Thanks for singing to me.' And then what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We bought a hat for you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and Juliesta said, 'Oh, a hat for me? Thank you! I have lots of hats at home but I don't have one here. Could I try it on?' And Hattie said, 'Yes.' And Hattie booped it right onto Juliesta's head and she said, 'Oh, it fits perfectly. Thank you. But I was not upset because I didn't have a hat. I was upset about something else.' And Mole said, 'Well what were you so upset about?' and Juliesta said, 'Actually, it's because I was kind of nervous about something. I'm still kind of nervous about it. It helps to have you guys here and singing to me and bringing me hats and I feel a little better, but I'm still nervous because I have to put on a show later. Do you think you could help me with that?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm all done stories now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All done? Okay, what do you want to do next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Diaper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Diaper? Okay. Do you have to use the potty?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Do you have some pee-pee?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Mickey
characters.description: One of Mistress Mouse’s storied stuffy mice, this famous plush adventurer is a visionary benefactor who manifests fragrant blooms from magic Legos and pilots his Mousecar through lightless depths. Whether arming moles for Kitty’s investigations or securing solutions for Frasgle, he remains fiercely loyal to Minnie, Mole, Izzy, and Hattie. Accompanied by a cheerful infant and shadowed by a friend’s disappearance, his haste to reach the spires of Peacock’s castle has left him without essential provisions, miring him in a sudden, hungry peril.

characters.name: Minnie
characters.description: A wise veteran of briny depths and interstellar frontiers, Mickey’s resourceful mouse companion and confidant to the Princess of Mouseland guides Izzy and Hattie with a glowing lantern and ingenious Mystery Mousketools. Having recently breathed life into a towering guardian of interlocking bricks, this intrepid traveler now points the way toward the mountain’s peak, pausing to investigate the savory aroma of bacon-scented mushrooms while serving as a compassionate friend whose seasoned instincts help decipher Frasgle’s complex roars and sounds.

characters.name: Mole
characters.description: A resourceful, perpetually humming mole with an impossibly long name, this polite and intrepid mariner serves as a bridge between worlds, translating Frasgle’s unique tongue and guiding the way to the hidden Underground Library. Whether managing rocket controls with wide-eyed curiosity, unraveling treehouse mysteries, or sharing the magic of special Legos, he encourages his companions to embrace the unknown between tending his dandelion yard and baking fragrant banana bread. A lover of both wriggly worms and sweet blueberries, he sings whimsical songs of carnabee sharks and drives a noisy dark brown car, remaining firmly hesitant about boarding giant robots even as he coaxes his friends toward bold new adventures.

characters.name: Juliesta
characters.description: A yellow-furred cousin of Frazzle who is nervous about an upcoming performance.

places.name: Hattie's Garden
places.description: A yard featuring a lovely bench where friends gather to chat.

places.name: Hattie's Workshop
places.description: Tucked within the cozy hollow of a weathered tree, Hattie Mouse’s dwelling serves as a busy, imaginative atelier and social hub where the air hums with the rhythmic snip of scissors. Here, amidst a cheerful clutter of measuring tapes, wet pigments, and scavenged shells, she crafts her signature starry hats from an eclectic mix of fur and feathers. The workshop is bathed in the earthy radiance of her fungal store—a brilliant collection of red, orange, and yellow mushrooms—illuminating the haunting indigo witch painting where Hattie first encountered the squeaking worm. It is in this storied corner, beneath the witch's gaze, that she recently unearthed a mysterious message hidden alongside a cache of sparkly green clover stickers.

places.name: The Treehouse
places.description: A high-up play place accessed by swinging from a neighboring tree branch.
